name: Chess.com Analysis
positioning: Board-first analysis with a broad consumer funnel into lessons, puzzles, and practice.
surface_notes: Strong left-nav ecosystem keeps analysis adjacent to puzzles, lessons, and training.
Analysis surface is immediately usable without extra setup.
Feels optimized for habit-forming daily use rather than deep editorial context.
opportunities_for_narrative_chess: Keep the board as the primary task surface.
Make it obvious how players get from a move to a deeper learning mode.

name: Lichess Analysis Board
positioning: Immediate, no-friction analysis and study tooling with a stripped-down utility feel.
surface_notes: Extremely fast path from landing on the page to making moves.
Board, engine panel, and PGN/FEN inputs all stay visible together.
Visual language is restrained and functional rather than brand-heavy.
opportunities_for_narrative_chess: Preserve this level of legibility when the narrative layer is on.
Avoid forcing story UI to compete with the board.

name: Aimchess
positioning: Performance analytics and personalized lessons generated from your own games.
surface_notes: The product promise is personalized improvement, not generic content.
Landing page leans heavily on clean product illustration and progress framing.
opportunities_for_narrative_chess: Narrative moments can become a coaching lens, not just flavor text.
Surface recurring player patterns and story beats over time.

name: DecodeChess
positioning: Explainable engine analysis focused on the why behind recommended moves.
surface_notes: Clear 'AI tutor' framing differentiates it from standard engine tools.
Tabs like threats, plans, concepts, and functionality make analysis feel teachable.
Product message is strongest when it translates analysis into human language.
opportunities_for_narrative_chess: Tie narrative text to concrete chess explanations instead of free-floating prose.
Use story only where it improves understanding of plans, threats, or momentum.

name: Chessable
positioning: Memory-driven opening and course study with a strong instructor and catalog model.
surface_notes: Hero message is direct about retention, openings, and science-backed learning.
Marketplace scale and instructor credibility are part of the core pitch.
Less about live board utility, more about repeatable structured study.
opportunities_for_narrative_chess: Package authored city/story scenarios as learnable study content later.
Keep the current prototype focused on usable board interactions before expanding course-like systems.
